seo.meta_title: Auth.js vs Logto for Next.js App Router (2026): Which Auth Tool Wins?
seo.meta_description: Auth.js or Logto for Next.js authentication? Compare features, pricing, learning curves, and find out which auth tool fits your 2026 project.
seo.og_title: Auth.js vs Logto: Next.js Auth Comparison (2026)
seo.og_description: Auth.js scores 8/10 for Next.js DX. Logto scores 7/10. See how they compare on pricing, features, and setup time.

hero.headline: Auth.js vs Logto for Next.js Authentication
hero.subheadline: An honest comparison of Auth.js and Logto — pricing, DX, setup time, and which one we recommend for Next.js App Router projects in 2026.

quick_summary: Auth.js vs Logto is a common comparison we hear from Next.js developers. Auth.js (4.6★) and Logto (4.1★) both have loyal followings. Our analysis shows Auth.js has the edge for most Next.js projects due to its 8/10 DX score. Logto is a strong alternative if yes, up to 5,000 mau better matches your budget.
verdict_text: We recommend Auth.js for most Next.js App Router projects. Its medium learning curve and 20 minutes setup time make it immediately productive. Logto is a worthy alternative, particularly if you need yes, up to 5,000 mau or prefer Logto's approach to authentication.

pros: Massive community with extensive documentation and examples
Framework-agnostic but excellent Next.js support via next-auth
Supports 80+ OAuth providers out of the box
Battle-tested in production across thousands of applications
cons: No built-in UI components - you build your own login pages
Configuration can become complex for advanced use cases
Database setup required for persistent sessions

pros: Built-in management API and admin console
Support for social sign-in, MFA, and passwordless auth
User-friendly admin dashboard for non-technical team members
cons: Smaller community and fewer third-party integrations
Next.js SDK is less mature with fewer examples
Self-hosted version requires significant infrastructure setup

faq.question: Should I choose Auth.js or Logto for my Next.js app?
faq.answer: It depends on your specific needs. Auth.js scores 8/10 for Next.js integration with a Medium learning curve. Logto scores 7/10 with a Medium learning curve. For most projects, we recommend Auth.js because of its stronger DX and better alignment with App Router patterns. However, if Yes, fully open source or Yes, up to 5,000 MAU is a deciding factor, the cheaper option may serve you well.

faq.question: Is Auth.js or Logto more affordable?
faq.answer: Auth.js starts at Free (Open Source) with a free tier of Yes, fully open source. Logto starts at Free – $599/mo with a free tier of Yes, up to 5,000 MAU. The total cost of ownership depends on your MAU (monthly active users). At lower volumes, the free tiers of both are sufficient. As you scale, Logto offers better value at scale. Factor in hidden costs like enterprise features, SSO, and database infrastructure when comparing.

faq.question: Is Auth.js or Logto easier to set up with Next.js?
faq.answer: Auth.js has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 20 minutes. Logto has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 20 minutes. Logto is faster to set up, but ease of integration also depends on your team's familiarity with the framework and whether you want pre-built UI components or prefer to build your own.

faq.question: Can I use Auth.js and Logto together?
faq.answer: Using both Auth.js and Logto in the same Next.js application is not recommended. They would create separate session management systems, conflicting middleware, and competing cookies. Choose one auth provider and stick with it. Migrating between auth providers later is possible but requires user data export and rebuilding authentication flows.

faq.question: Which has better developer experience: Auth.js or Logto?
faq.answer: Auth.js has a Medium learning curve, a Next.js integration score of 8/10, and a user rating of 4.6★. Logto has a Medium learning curve, 7/10, and 4.1★. On paper, Auth.js leads in DX. In practice, your specific requirements around pre-built components, customization, and team expertise matter just as much. Try both free tiers to see what clicks.
